summary: Drew DeVault provides his May 2020 status update covering progress across his major projects. The Wayland Protocol book has been made freely available under CC-BY-SA, while wlroots and Sway continue receiving steady bugfixes. On the email front, aerc gets incremental improvements and he's joined Simon Ser to work on Alps, a lightweight webmail client. SourceHut had a particularly productive month with Plan 9 CI support, a new GraphQL API for git.sr.ht, and the long-awaited project hub launch.
key_points: The Wayland Protocol book is now free to read and relicensed under CC-BY-SA, with wlroots and Sway in a mature, stable maintenance phase
Alps, a lightweight webmail client, is being prepared for production use by Migadu
SourceHut added Plan 9 support to its CI, claimed as a world first for any CI platform
SourceHut launched its project hub and an experimental GraphQL API for git.sr.ht
money_quotes: The Wayland Protocol is now free for anyone to read, and has been relicensed as CC-BY-SA.
wlroots continues to enjoy incremental improvements, and is being refined further and further towards a perfect citizen of the ecosystem in which it resides.
Both have been been stable for a while now: the only meaningful changes will be, for the most part, a steady stream of bug fixes and performance improvements.
We've added Plan 9 support to the CI — a world first :D
I also just published the first bits of the new, experimental GraphQL API for git.sr.ht.
key_insight: A productive month across DeVault's ecosystem shows mature Wayland projects settling into stability while SourceHut pushes boundaries with Plan 9 CI support and new APIs.
